description: Recreate the sky over the Berlin Observatory (52.503 N, 13.393 E, 35m) on the night of Neptune's discovery (September 23, 1846, at 23:00:00 UTC). Configure the display for an astronomical chart view by disabling the atmosphere and ground, and enabling constellation lines, names, boundaries, and the equatorial grid. Locate Neptune, center the view, and save the current application settings. Document the historical position by creating a file at /home/ga/Desktop/neptune_1846.txt containing the location name (Berlin), the discovery date, the constellation where Neptune was located, and its approximate Right Ascension (RA).
